rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:21: 'CLI or desktop, your call.' — platform correctly scoped to CLI/Desktop; no claude.ai-only features invoked.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:85 (maintainer): 'Confirmed against 2026-04 Anthropic Claude Code setup docs + Microsoft devblogs' — capability claims are footnoted in maintainer block with verification sourcing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — write 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:7: 'Fire up Claude Code on the laptop you'll use during the training.' uses launch framing. line:21: 'CLI or desktop, your call. Open a new session with the repo as the working directory.' is correct. Section 0 launch framing does not conflate session/app in a way that breaks pedagogy — but rule 7 flags the phrasing.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Change line 7 to 'Start Claude Code on the laptop you'll use during the training.' — preserves launch meaning while honoring rule 7; §2 already correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pseudo-URI + kill-switch for public/private content split.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No cross-repo private-link in student-facing body. <CONTENT_URL> is a build-time substitution, not a pseudo-URI reference to private content.

line:36: '<CONTENT_URL>' appears inside the fenced block at step 3.b — maintainer block line:86 documents explicit build-time substitution exception ('covered by build-time substitution, not student fill-in'). Carve-out is correctly scoped and documented.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:29: '**3.b. Or have Claude download it for you.** Ask Claude to fetch the content tarball to ~/Downloads/.' — action lead-in with command verb 'Ask Claude to fetch' immediately precedes the **Prompt** label at line:31. Prior REVISE finding (mechanical narration 'Paste this prompt instead:') is resolved by this edit.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:31-33 and line:45-47: Both prompt blocks use '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*' label with blank line before fence. Action lead-in present before each label.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines:55-56 inside fenced block: '- access-control-analysis' and '- stride' — skill names only, no path. No 'content/skills/' or '~/.claude/skills/' path appears inside any fenced block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight in Claude Code.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No **bold** or *italic* markdown inside either fenced block. Uses plain bullets and numbered lists inside fences.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check — every named tool, skill, connector, file, or path a prompt invokes must exist.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Skills 'access-control-analysis' and 'stride' verified installed at ~/.claude/skills/ with SKILL.md present. Content tarball ~/Downloads/agents-102-content.tar.gz is the student-produced artifact from step 3. ~/Documents/ae101-content/ is created by the prompt itself (step 1). All invocations trace to installable or self-created artifacts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — and forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Core pedagogical move — bug screening with criteria (lines 60-61) — lives inside the fenced prompt block, not in surrounding body prose. Body prose describes the transport mechanic (step 3), not a scripted reaction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework does not build verification machinery.

line:85 (maintainer): 'Skills install to ~/.claude/skills/ (user-level), auto-discover in every session regardless of cwd.' — prework installs skills at the correct lazy-load tier; auto-load tier not claimed for skills.

No practitioner citations or statistics in student-facing body. Maintainer block references 'Anthropic Claude Code setup docs + Microsoft devblogs' for platform claims — in maintainer block only, consistent with §1 scope.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Fire up Claude Code on the laptop you'll use during the training.
CLI or desktop, your call. Open a new session with the repo as the working directory.
Claude will need to write outside the repo (~Documents/ae101-content/ and ~/.claude/skills/); approve the permission prompts when they fire.
Skills install to ~/.claude/skills/ (user-level), auto-discover in every session regardless of cwd.
Cross-platform via Claude Code's Bash tool: macOS/Linux native, Windows via Git Bash (which Claude Code requires).

urls_sampled.note: Build-time placeholder — not a live URL in source; substituted by scripts/build-workbook.js at render time. Exempt from liveness check.
